subject: Social Science
chapter_name: Social and Political Life – II: Understanding Media
overall_learning_objectives: Identify different types of media and their roles
Understand media influence on society and public opinion
Develop skills to verify information and spot misinformation
Enhance research, critical thinking, and presentation skills

lesson_plan.title: Types of Media and Their Roles
topics_covered: Role of media in education and awareness
Examples of media impact on society
teaching_strategy: Show examples of newspapers, TV news clips, and social media posts
Class discussion on which media they use and why
student_activities: List media types they encounter daily
Discuss one way each type influences people

lesson_plan.title: Analyzing News Reports
topics_covered: Headlines, main story, and supporting details
student_activities: Highlight facts and opinions in a short news article
assessment: Accuracy in identifying facts and opinions

lesson_plan.title: Media Bias and Perspective
topics_covered: Comparing multiple sources on the same topic
teaching_strategy: Show two news articles reporting the same event differently
Class discussion on differences and possible reasons
student_activities: Compare articles and identify differences in coverage
Explain which article seems more objective and why

lesson_plan.title: Spotting Fake News
topics_covered: What is misinformation and fake news
teaching_strategy: Provide a mix of real and fake news examples
Demonstrate verification methods using credible sources
student_activities: Check one social media post or article for authenticity
Discuss the method used to verify it

lesson_plan.title: Mini Media Project
teaching_strategy: Guide students on structuring a short news report
student_activities: Prepare a 5–6 sentence news story about a school or local event

lesson_plan.title: Media and Society Reflection
topics_covered: Recap media types, verification, and bias
Discuss media’s influence on opinions and behavior
teaching_strategy: Class discussion linking lessons to students’ daily media use
Ask reflective questions about media influence
student_activities: Write one reflection on how they can use media responsibly

teaching_aids: Newspapers, online news articles, TV news clips
Charts and diagrams explaining media structure
Sample social media posts for analysis
Notebook and pen for writing activities
teacher_tip: Encourage students to compare multiple sources, ask critical questions, and verify information. Use real-life examples and online media to make lessons relevant and interactive.
